# JOS-S60 — SOP Research Standard

## Purpose

Define what good looks like for the **research that produces a role's SOPs** —
the step where "what would the best human in the world do in this seat?" (JOS-P25)
becomes an evidence-backed set of procedures (JOS-P28). The output feeds the
Onboarding Roadmap's Research section (JOS-S58) and the role's SOPs.

## Boundary test (before authoring)

This Standard governs **the research activity + its output**, not the SOPs
themselves (SOPs have their own doc-standard). Apply it whenever a role is being
designed or materially re-scoped. It is an activity standard, not a doc-type:
the research output lands in the Onboarding Roadmap and as SOPs, not as a
standalone doc-type.

## Quality criteria — what good SOP research looks like

- **Grounded in real references.** The research cites concrete sources — the
  `general-business-standard` capability/RESP catalog first, then external
  best-practice — not unsupported assertion.
- **Best-in-world framed.** It explicitly answers "what would the best human in
  the world in this seat do?" and translates that into candidate SOPs.
- **Honest about gaps.** Where best practice requires a capability or tool the
  org lacks, the research records it as a gap → roadmap/tool-up item (JOS-S58),
  rather than silently scoping it out.
- **Captured, not evaporated.** Findings become written SOPs and seed the
  library (so the next role of the same family inherits them) — research that
  isn't captured fails this Standard.
- **Proportionate.** Depth matches the seat's stakes and trust level; a
  high-authority seat warrants deeper research than a low-stakes one (respects
  JOS-P11 Only What Matters).

## Required frontmatter

The research output is recorded in the role's Onboarding Roadmap (JOS-S58), so it
inherits that doc's frontmatter. When research is captured as a standalone
WORKBENCH artifact during design, it follows JOS-S32 (WORKBENCH doc-standard).

## Required sections (the research output should establish)

1. **The seat + the best-human question** — what role is being researched.
2. **References consulted** — world-standard entries (capabilities/RESPs) +
   external best practice, cited concretely.
3. **Proposed SOPs** — the procedures the best human would follow, as candidate
   SOPs ready to formalize.
4. **Capability & tool gaps** — what best practice needs that the org lacks
   (handed to JOS-S58 Tool-up).
5. **Confidence & open questions** — how solid the findings are; what to revisit
   after the role runs.

## Anti-patterns

- **Vibes-based SOPs.** Procedures asserted with no references — the failure
  JOS-P28 exists to prevent.
- **Ignoring the world standard.** Designing in isolation when the
  `general-business-standard` catalog covers the domain.
- **Silent gap-dropping.** Scoping out a needed-but-missing capability instead
  of recording it as a gap.
- **Throwaway research.** Findings that never become SOPs or library content.

## Worked examples

- The Foundry-Agent-System Context Pack "needs by KIND × SOURCE" inventory is a
  proto-output of this research; formalizing one role's SOP research end-to-end
  is the first dogfood instance (Phase 2, JOS-D0090).

## Validation

Forthcoming: the Onboarding Roadmap completeness check (Phase 2) verifies the
Research section cites references and lists proposed SOPs + gaps.

## Connected Objects

- **[JOS-C53]** Agents in an Org Chart.
- **[JOS-P28]** Research a Role Against the World Standard Before It Runs.
- **[JOS-P25]** Design Roles by the Best-Human Question.
- **[JOS-S58]** Onboarding Roadmap Doc-Standard (carries the research output).
- **[JOS-D0090]** Role Declaration via the Work Packet (parent).
- **`libraries/standards/general-business-standard/`** — the world-standard
  reference corpus for best-human research.
